description: In a formal venue with people in the background, Rachel wears a voluminous pink dress and a large matching hat. Standing near Ross, who wears a tuxedo, Rachel looks forward and says, "I had to sing the Copa Cabana in front of the entire school. I think I got two lines into it before I ran and"

Rachel grabs the brim of her hat with both hands and pulls it down slightly over her face, adding, "freaked out. Oh, my God. My entire life's flashing before my eyes." Ross looks at her and says, "Rach." Faint instrumental music plays in the background.
Ross steps slightly closer to Rachel, gesturing with his hands as he says, "Hey, look. I remember that. It wasn't so bad."
Rachel lowers her hands, turning toward Ross. Her eyebrows draw together as she says, "Oh, Ross, would you stop? You got me. I'm dating you."
An older couple approaches them.
The older woman says, "Rachel." Rachel turns to face them and replies, "Oh, hi, Mr. Weinberg. Hi, Mrs."
Mrs. Weinberg smiles and says, "Weinberg. It's so wonderful to see you again, my dear. In fact, I hardly expected to see so much." Mr. Weinberg stands beside her, smiling.
Rachel lowers her gaze briefly. Ross looks at Mr. Weinberg.
Mr. Weinberg turns his head toward Mrs. Weinberg and says, "You told me you didn't see anything."
Mrs. Weinberg replies to him, "I tell you a lot of things."
Ross gestures vaguely with his hand.
Mrs. Weinberg pats Rachel's arm and says, "Well, it's wonderful to have you up and about again, dear. Stay well." The couple turns to walk away.
Rachel watches them leave, then turns her gaze back to Ross.
Rachel speaks to Ross, her eyebrows raised, "Okay. Now that is the third time someone has said something like that to me today."
Ross says, "Rach." Behind them, a woman in a white wedding dress and veil enters through wooden double doors.
The woman rushes forward with her arms outstretched. Rachel turns, gasps, and they grasp each other's hands. The woman says, "Rach. Oh, my God. I'm married. I'm Mrs. Dr. Barry Hunter."
Rachel and the woman, Mindy, continue holding hands and smiling. A man, Barry, wearing a grey tuxedo and a top hat walks through the double doors behind them.
Barry approaches them, checks his wristwatch, and speaks. Mindy links her arm through his.
Ross laughs and points toward Barry. Rachel and Mindy smile, and Barry chuckles.
Rachel looks at the couple and says, "I have a question for you guys."
Rachel continues, "Why do people keep saying it's good to see me up and about?"
Barry and Mindy exchange a brief look.
Mindy gestures with her free hand and says, "Well, after you ran out on your wedding Barry's parents told people you were sort of insane." Barry looks away slightly.
Rachel stares at them, her mouth closed.
Rachel asks, her eyebrows drawn together, "Insane?"
Mindy clarifies, "From the syphilis? What"
Rachel's mouth drops open. Ross stares ahead.
Barry gestures with his hand and says, "were they going to say? You didn't love me anymore? Come on. Your choices were that or syphilis."
Ross looks at Rachel, extending his hand toward her as he says, "Well, hey, sweetie. At least this puts your whole tushy show into perspective."
Rachel turns her gaze to Ross, her face unsmiling.
The scene changes to an apartment living area. Joey holds a large portable phone to his ear and says, "Angela. Joey Tribbiani."
Joey turns and begins to pace.
Joey walks back and forth, saying into the phone, "Listen, what are you doing tonight? I know you're seeing that guy. I was thinking maybe you could bring him." Chandler sits in the background.
Joey stops pacing.
Joey pulls the phone slightly away from his ear and asks, "Hello?"
Joey says "Hello" again, lowers the phone, and walks toward a carved wooden statue standing on the floor.
Joey lifts the statue with both hands and carries it out of the room through an open doorway.
In the kitchen, Chandler sits staring at a laptop while Phoebe stands beside him, chopping vegetables. The laptop emits a double chime sound.

Phoebe looks at Chandler and says, "Aren't you going to answer her? That's like the 10th bing bong message she sent. She wants to know what's wrong. What's wrong?" Chandler rests his hand over his mouth.
Chandler turns toward the laptop and says loudly, "What's wrong? You're married."
Chandler pushes his chair back and stands up, saying, "That's what's wrong."
Chandler turns away from the counter, placing his hands on his hips. Phoebe watches him.
Phoebe covers her mouth with her hand and says, "Oh, my. What?" Chandler turns his head back toward her.
Chandler faces Phoebe.
Phoebe points her knife toward him and says, "She wants to meet you in person."
Chandler gestures with both hands and replies, his voice louder, "Hey, look, Phoebe, I wanted to meet her in person too, okay? But she's married. She has a husband."
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she speaks, "What if the husband person is the wrong guy and you are the right guy? You don't get chances like this all the"
Phoebe continues, "time. If you don't meet her now, you're going to be kicking yourself when you're 80 which is hard to do. And that's how you break a hip."
Chandler looks away, exhaling audibly and shifting his stance.
Chandler looks down and says softly, "I don't know."
Phoebe looks at him. The laptop chimes again.
Phoebe gestures toward the laptop with her knife and says, "Listen to that. Those are the bing bongs of a woman in love with you."
Chandler stares ahead, his posture rigid.
Chandler sets his jaw, raises a fist briefly, and says, "Okay, I'll do it."
Phoebe smiles widely, clapping her hands, and says rapidly, "Oh, yay. Okay, great. Go put on your shoes and then march out there and"
Phoebe waves her arms, moving around Chandler as she says, "meet her. Oh, my God. No, wait. You have to take a shower because EW. And I'll get no, you have to answer her. Answer"
Phoebe gestures frantically with her hands, continuing, "her first. No, you know what? Make some coffee because it's too much." Chandler stands near her, his brow furrowed.
The scene changes to a banquet hall where a band plays lively music. A woman in a dark dress walks past a table where a man with a mustache, Richard, is seated.
Rachel stands at a microphone in front of the band, saying, "Okay, one more, please. Come on, I'm going to get it in this time. I will."
A band member in a white suit stands behind her and says, "Okay, last chance. When she passes"
Rachel nods slightly, her gaze fixed forward as the band continues playing.

Inside a brightly lit banquet hall, guests sit at round tables covered with pink tablecloths. A man with a mustache, Richard, sits beside Monica, who wears a sleeveless floral dress. An older woman at a nearby table looks toward them.
Richard leans slightly forward and says, "again, I'm sorry." The older woman waves a hand toward him and turns her head away.
Richard and Monica turn to face each other. Monica rests her hands near a pink napkin on the table.
Monica says, "You know what?"
Monica gestures with her hands as she speaks, "Maybe I don't need to have children. No. Maybe. I just think I do. Because that's what society and by that I mean my mom"
Monica continues to speak, her head tilting slightly.
Two young girls in dresses run past the table holding hands. Monica continues, "has always convinced me that I want her so badly."
Richard looks toward the girls as they pass, and Monica's gaze follows them.
Monica turns back to Richard, clasping her hands, and says, "I do."
Monica leans slightly closer to Richard.
"I have to have children. I'm sorry," Monica says, waving her hands rapidly.
"I just do," Monica adds.
Richard looks at her, nodding slightly.
At a long head table, a man in a tuxedo, Joel, stands up and taps his glass with a utensil. "Yo, can I have your attention, please?" he asks loudly.
The room quiets as Joel looks around.
"Best man making a toast here," Joel announces.
"Thank you," Joel says.
Joel pulls a small card from his pocket and looks at it.
Joel reads, "I remember when Barry got home from his first date with Rachel."
At the head table, Rachel, wearing a bright pink puffy dress, and Ross, in a tuxedo, look up sharply. The crowd murmurs audibly.
Joel looks off-screen and asks, "What? You hired the same band? I can't use the same speech."
Joel raises his glass.
"Thank you. Thank you very much," Joel says.
Joel tucks the card away.
"Anyway, I wish you both a wonderful life together," Joel continues, gesturing with his free hand.
Joel looks back at his card.
"And Rachel," Joel says. "What?" Rachel asks, her volume slightly raised.
Joel says, "No, no. In all seriousness there's not a lot of women who would have had the guts to come back here tonight. And"
"even fewer who do it with their asses hanging out," Joel finishes.
The wedding guests laugh and gasp. Rachel looks down at her dress, pressing her lips together with her eyes widened.
Ross stands up from his seat beside Rachel, tapping his glass with a fork.
"I'd like to add something to that," Ross says loudly. "What are you adding? Why are you adding what?" Rachel whispers rapidly, leaning toward him.
Ross looks out at the guests.
"Most of you don't know me," Ross says.
"I'm Rachel's boyfriend," Ross states loudly. "Oh, dear God," Rachel murmurs softly, covering her eyes with one hand.
Rachel keeps her hand over her eyes.
"Ross," Rachel whispers softly.
Ross continues loudly, "And I'd just like to say that it did take a lot of courage for Rachel to come here tonight."
Ross pauses, shifting his weight.
"And just for the record, she did not run out on Barry because she had syphilis," Ross states loudly. A drummer behind him immediately plays a brief rimshot rhythm.
Rachel stares at Ross, her mouth slightly open.
"What are you doing?" Rachel whispers. Ross looks back at the drummer and says loudly, "I'm serious."
Ross turns his attention back to the room.
"The reason she walked out on Barry is simply that she didn't love him," Ross explains, gesturing with his free hand.
Ross lowers his hand.
"Which, incidentally, worked out pretty well for me," Ross says loudly.
The drummer plays another rimshot rhythm. Ross looks at the drummer and smiles, nodding slightly.
Rachel stares fixedly at Ross, her brow furrowed, leaning away from him as he adjusts his posture.
"So to review," Ross says loudly.
Ross pauses, looking around the room.
"Rachel took a lot of courage," Ross states.
Ross raises his glass slightly.
"Doesn't have the syphilis," Ross continues.
Rachel rests her forehead against her hand, looking down.
"And wound up with me," Ross says loudly.
Ross smiles toward the guests.
"Cheers," Ross says, raising his glass higher.
Ross drinks from his glass. Rachel stands up abruptly, pushing her chair back.
"See you in the parking lot," Rachel mutters as she steps away from the table.
"No, Rach," Ross says softly, lowering his glass as he watches her.
Rachel walks rapidly away from the head table. Joel steps back to the microphone.
"And once again, she is out of here. Okay, who had 9:45?" Joel says loudly, looking at his watch.
Guests in the room murmur and laugh. Richard and Monica turn their heads to watch Rachel walk past their table.
Rachel stops in the middle of the room, her back turned, and stands still. Ross watches her from the head table.
Rachel turns around and walks briskly toward the band's stage. She steps up to a microphone stand.
Rachel grips the microphone and says, "You know what, Bear? I'm not going to leave. I probably should, but I'm not."
Rachel continues, "Because I"
"promised myself I would make it through at least one of your weddings," Rachel finishes loudly, looking out at the room.
Rachel nods slightly, maintaining her gaze forward.
In a banquet hall, Rachel stands at a microphone on a small stage, wearing a large pink dress. Guests sit at round tables in the foreground.
Rachel gestures with one hand and says, "See, now, tonight, all I really wanted was to make it through this evening with a little bit of grace"
Rachel lowers her hand and pauses.
Rachel brings her hands together and says, "and dignity."
Rachel presses her lips together.
Rachel says, "Well, I guess we can all agree that's not going to happen," lifting her shoulders and spreading her arms. Several guests laugh.
Barry and Mindy smile at a table. Monica and Richard sit together at another table. Rachel rests her hands on her hips.
Rachel looks out at the guests and says, "I guess there's nothing really left to say"
Rachel lowers her gaze, keeping her hands on her hips.
Rachel looks up and says, "except"
Rachel closes her eyes and lowers her arms, standing still.
Rachel opens her eyes and touches the microphone. The band behind her begins to play an upbeat song.
Rachel sings, "her name was Lola" to the melody of the song. Ross stands near the stage, watching her.
Mindy and Barry look toward the stage.
Rachel sings, "she was a showgirl"
Rachel sways slightly while holding the microphone. Guests watch from their tables.
Rachel gestures near her head and then her chest, singing, "with yellow feather feathers in her hair and dress cut down to there"
Rachel looks toward Ross.
Ross prompts her off-mic, gesturing with his hand. Rachel says, "she would merengue thank you, honey."
Rachel turns back to the guests and sings, "And do the cha cha."
Rachel sings, "And while she'd like to be a star Tony Owens tended bar." She waves her hands upward and says, "Wait, everybody?" Ross joins her on stage, holding a pink rose.
The crowd sings along to the music. Ross steps beside Rachel, holding up the rose, and Rachel smiles, placing her arm around his waist.
Ross puts his arm around Rachel's shoulders, and they sway together side-to-side on the stage.
Rachel and Ross continue swaying as the band plays. Several guests smile and converse at their tables.
A large crystal chandelier hangs illuminated in the hall. The setting changes to the dance floor, where numerous guests are dancing.
Richard and Monica dance together among the crowd. Richard looks down at Monica.
Richard says, "Okay, I'll do it."
Monica asks, "You'll do what?"
Richard replies, "If kids is what it takes to be with you, then kids it is."
Monica looks at Richard with her mouth slightly open.
Monica says, "Oh, my God."
Richard maintains eye contact with Monica.
Richard says, "If I have to, I'll do it all again."
Richard continues, "I'll do the 4:00 feeding thing. I'll go to the PTA meetings."
Richard looks steadily at Monica.
Richard says, "I'll coach the soccer team."
Monica tilts her head slightly.
Monica asks, "Really?" Richard nods and says, "Yeah, if I have to."
Richard keeps his arms around Monica.
Richard says, "Monica,"
Monica gazes at Richard as they sway slowly to the music.
Richard says, "I don't want to lose you."
Richard briefly lowers his gaze, then looks back up.
Richard says, "So if I have to do it all again, then I will."
Monica smiles slightly, keeping her eyes on Richard. They continue to slowly move to the music.
Monica says, "You're the most wonderful man."
Monica raises her hand to rest on Richard's chest.
Monica says, "And if you hadn't have said If I have to, like, 17 times"
Monica looks at Richard.
Monica continues, "then I'd be saying okay, let's do it."
Richard holds his gaze on Monica.
Richard says, "But you're not."
Monica looks away briefly, her smile fading, then returns her gaze to Richard.
Monica says, "Oh, my God."
Monica lowers her chin and breathes out.
Monica says, "I can't believe what I'm getting ready to say."
Monica takes a slow breath, her face remaining still.
Monica says, "I want to have a baby."
Richard looks back at her silently.
Monica says, "But I don't want to have one with someone who doesn't really want to have one."
Richard swallows and keeps his eyes fixed on Monica.
Richard says, "I."
Richard's jaw tightens slightly as he continues looking at Monica. The ambient music continues.
Monica gazes up at Richard, her eyes visibly wet.
Richard says, "I love you."
Monica smiles softly, maintaining eye contact.
Monica says, "I know you do."
Richard presses his lips together slightly.
Richard says, "Me too."
Monica rests her head on Richard's shoulder, and he wraps his arms securely around her back.
An off-screen singer sings, "Tell." Monica and Richard sway together in the large room.
Monica and Richard continue to sway.
The singer continues, "to put on." Monica rests her head on Richard's chest.
They maintain their position.
Monica lifts her head slightly and says, "So what now?"
Richard turns his head slightly.
Richard looks forward and says, "I guess we just"
They continue to sway.
Richard continues, "keep dancing."
Richard rests his cheek against Monica's head.
The singer sings, "Someone." They continue to sway.
They keep dancing as the singer performs on stage behind them.
They sway as other dancing couples fill the large room.
A city skyline with illuminated skyscrapers is shown at night.
Inside a busy coffee shop, Joey sits at a small table, while Ross sits on a sofa. Chandler walks in rapidly, looking around. "Where is she? Where is she? Oh, hey, I have a question. Where is she?"
Rachel steps toward Chandler and says, "Chandler."
Rachel holds Chandler by the arms. She says, "Relax. Chandler. She'll be"
Chandler steps away from Rachel and looks toward the front window.
Chandler points toward the window and says, "Oh, that's her." A blonde woman walks past outside. Ross, looking up from his newspaper, says, "Yeah, because life's just that kind."
Chandler lowers his hand and looks away from the window.
Phoebe, sitting in an armchair with a mug, gestures with one hand. "Chandler, you gotta stop staring at the door. It's like a watched pot. You know? If you keep looking at it, then the door"
Phoebe continues, "is never going to boil."
Chandler turns back toward the room.
Chandler turns toward Phoebe and begins to speak. "I think what you have to do is try not to"
Chandler stops speaking mid-sentence, turning his gaze toward the entrance.
Chandler stares toward the entrance, his eyes widening.
Chandler stares toward the door and says, "Oh, my God."
A woman with dark curly hair enters the coffee shop. She stops, places her hands over her chest, and gasps.
The woman says, "Oh,"
She says, "my"
She keeps her hands on her chest.
She says, "God."
Chandler stares at her, his posture straight.
Chandler walks quickly toward the woman.
Chandler and the woman embrace and kiss passionately.
They continue to kiss in the doorway.
Joey, Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe all turn toward the couple and say in unison, "Oh, my God."
Chandler and the woman continue kissing.
The screen turns black, displaying white text: "EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS KEVIN S. BRIGHT MARTA KAUFFMAN DAVID CRANE".

In an apartment living room, Joey reclines in a chair holding a script. Ross walks in briskly and says, "All right, I've been feeling incredibly guilty about this" On-screen text lists production credits.
Ross stops near Joey's chair and continues, "because I want to be a good friend. And damn it, I am a good friend. So just"
Ross leans toward Joey and says, "shut up and close your eyes."
Ross kisses Joey on the lips. Joey keeps his eyes closed. Text identifying a special guest star appears.
Ross pulls away. Joey opens his eyes and says, "Wow,"
Joey looks up at Ross.
Joey says, "you are a good friend."
Joey continues, "Of course, the audition was this morning and I didn't get it."
Ross stares at Joey, his mouth opening slightly. Text listing the director of photography appears.
Joey points at Ross and says, "But that was a hell of kiss. Rachel is a very lucky girl."
Ross remains silent, staring at Joey. Joey looks down at his script. Production credits continue to appear.
A Warner Bros. Television copyright screen is displayed on a black background.
The copyright screen remains visible.
